calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Perl appeared by zoom for Barbara Headman (Petitioner).

The court stated that it did not see a response to the probate examiners notes, and that it is trying to understand the motivation for this petition because it appears that this account was held in joint tenancy and there is no need to do anything with probate court in order for Ms. Perl's client to obtain the proceeds. Ms. Perl apologized for the lack of response, stated that her family had been ill and that her client is asserting that title of joint tenancy is inaccurate. Ms. Perl also stated that it's really community property, that her client would benefit more from a community property full step up rather than a joint tenancy step up, that the account was open after the marriage and that the funds that went into the account were community property. In addition, Ms. Perl stated that her client will assert that title of joint tenancy was taken out of convenience not really understanding the implications. The court stated that it is not convinced that this is an appropriate use of a spousal property petition, that it understands that there are tough ramifications that they are trying to work through and that barring some kind of showing that this was an error, it is not going to get involved in using a spousal property petition to take this action. The Petition for Spousal Property is denied.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.
